Here is the caption to process:
"I've seen how addiction and wrong choices destroy lives. Now, I get to be part of their restoration instead of enforcement, helping individuals find healing, hope, and a new beginning." Steven Kaliaperumal, Centre Director at Teen Challenge Halfway House, brings both experience and heart to every resident's journey. As a former police officer, he understands the impact of addiction and poor choices, and now he walks alongside individuals as they rebuild their lives. Seeing lives transformed is what truly keeps him going.Under his leadership, the team continues to make Teen Challenge Halfway House a more welcoming space for healing. From creating warm communal areas and more green spaces to exploring new ways for residents to learn and work, these changes reflect the shared belief that dignity and hope should be part of rehabilitation.With leaders like Steven and a team committed to growing alongside their residents, no one walks this journey alone. Every step forward is a reminder that with guidance, care, and faith, second chances can become new beginnings.SPS SingaporePrisonService BeyondPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

Meet SGT Tang Chi Cong, who went from working as an Auxiliary Police Officer at where he was deployed at SPS to conduct security checks for visitors and vehicles, to joining as a CaptainOfLives!For him, every day is a learning opportunity, and he values the interactions he has with both his colleagues and inmates under his care. As a member of various Institution B3 Staff Well-Being initiatives and programmes, he always keeps a lookout for those in need, whether in a professional or personal capacity. That's his story. What will yours be?If you are ready for a challenging yet rewarding career, become a Captain of Lives today. Here is the caption to process:
"Why help? Why not."Meet Muzdalifah Binte Anuar or Efah, a mum of three, former SAF officer, Head of Home at a girls' orphanage, and a firm believer in second chances. Growing up, Efah didn't always walk the straight path. But along the way, people chose to see more than her mistakes and those second chances shaped the person she is today. Inspired by her father, a long-time prison religious counsellor, she now pays it forward by volunteering with the Singapore Prison Service, journeying with ex-offenders as they rebuild their lives.Despite a full schedule, volunteering grounds her. It's in the quiet conversations, the listening ear, the simple "thank you for coming" moments that remind her that every interaction is one filled with hope, regret, and love for their family."You don't need to be perfect. Just show up with a willing heart. Sometimes, believing in someone is the second chance they've been waiting for."Show her your support in the comments!SPS SingaporePrisonService BeyondPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

Co-developed by SPS and , the YRCP Portal serves as an integrated platform that consolidates all volunteer-facing digital services into a single, user-friendly interface where YRCP volunteers can access all relevant information, track and monitor engagements through customised dashboards and offer multiple communication channels to deliver effective and coordinated care to the families they serve. A heartfelt thank you, , and the grassroots divisions, and YRCP volunteers for your continued efforts in supporting the inmates families. Learning and competing with the best! From 22 to 26 September, SPS proudly hosted the 7th Asian Prisons Lockdown Challenge (APLC) 2025! This years edition welcomed eight teams, including three local teams comprising our CaptainsOfLives. The event challenges included:Team Obstacles Challenge Sharpshooter Challenge Operation Tech-Secure Challenge Rescue & Restore Challenge and the NEW Specialist Challenge, a first-ever female-only challenge Beyond the competition, participants also exchanged tactical knowledge through demonstrations and sharing sessions, reinforcing APLCs mission of strengthening regional networks and advancing correctional excellence. The APLC remains more than a competition it is a platform for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and capability-building that strengthens correctional systems across the region. Congratulations to Macao , Hong Kong and South Korea for clinching the top three spots in this years competition! AsianPrisonsLockdownChallenge APLC2025 SPS SingaporePrisonService
